title: [Polteageist] Battle Guide
summary: A [sing]le-turn setup [ghost] sweeper that turns 134 base Special Attack into a game-ending threat via [shell-smash].
strengths: One [shell-smash] pushes effective SpA past 260 and Speed past 140
Pure [Ghost] typing gives free immunity to [Normal] and [Fighting]
[cursed-body] can randomly [disable] the opponent's key attack

[shadow-sneak] and [sucker-punch] erase the sweep before it starts

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Polteageist] is a pure [Ghost] special sweeper built a[round] one idea: [shell-smash] plus [white-herb]. The 60/65/65/134/114/70 line is fragile, but the combo pushes effective Special Attack past 260 and Speed into the 140s, which is enough to clean most Champions MA teams in a [sing]le turn.

## Strengths
Base 134 SpA is already top-tier for a [Ghost], and after setup the numbers look like a Mega. [stored-power] at +2/+2 is a flat 140 BP STAB-like nuke, and [shadow-ball] handles whatever resists it. The pure [Ghost] typing hands [Polteageist] free switches against [Normal] and [Fighting] attackers, and base 114 SpD buys a [sing]le tanked special hit when it matters.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 60/65 physical bulk is the core problem — any neutral physical hit kills before setup. [Dark] and [Ghost] priority ([shadow-sneak], [sucker-punch]) is the textbook answer, so fast [Dark] attackers like Meowscarada and Kingambit force an immediate switch. Lo[sing] [white-herb] to [knock-off] before setup also dismantles the whole plan.

## Recommended Moveset
[shell-smash] is non-negotiable. [shadow-ball] is the locked STAB because the SpD drop snowballs the sweep. [stored-power] is the +2 finisher that cracks neutral walls, and [giga-drain] [round]s out the set by handling [Water], [Rock], and [Ground] switch-ins while [rest]oring HP. If the team already has grass cove[rage], swap [giga-drain] for [dark-pulse] to mirror other [Ghost]s.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[white-herb] is the default item because it cancels [shell-smash]'s defense drops on the same turn and lets [Polteageist] tank a follow-up. [focus-sash] is the alternative against heavy priority leads. The ability pick is [cursed-body] — it's a live [disable] on the opponent's key move that can [sing]le-handedly save a sweep. Tera [Fairy] flips the [Dark] weakness into a resistance, while Tera [Ghost] simply boosts the STAB ceiling.

## Teammates & Synergy
A pure [Ghost] needs dedicated answers to other [Dark] and [Ghost] attackers. [Fairy] and [Fighting] partners bait out the [Dark]s that wall [Polteageist], and a [stealth-rock] lead strips [focus-sash] from the oppo[sing] side so [stored-power] actually finishes kills. A [thunder-wave] or [sleep-powder] support makes the setup turn free, and an in[timid]ate pivot softens physical priority threats.

## Play Patterns
Do not lead [Polteageist]. Bring it in on turn two or three after the oppo[sing] physical wallbreaker has committed, set up behind a pivot, then clean. If the opponent looks like they will switch on the [shell-smash] turn, c[lick] [shadow-ball] for safe chip instead of [stored-power]. Save Tera for the moment a [Dark] move is telegraphed — flipping to [Fairy] that turn can invert the entire game.
